# permitio_role_assignment (Resource)

Assigns a role to a user within a specific tenant. This resource creates a tenant-level role assignment, granting the user permissions across all resources within the tenant.

For instance-level permissions (granting access to specific resource instances), use `permitio_resource_instance_role_assignment` instead.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Assign admin role to a user in the default tenant
resource "permitio_role_assignment" "admin" {
  user   = "user@example.com"
  role   = "admin"
  tenant = "default"
}

# Assign multiple roles using for_each
resource "permitio_role_assignment" "team_roles" {
  for_each = {
    "user1_admin"  = { user = "user1@example.com", role = "admin" }
    "user2_editor" = { user = "user2@example.com", role = "editor" }
  }

  user   = each.value.user
  role   = each.value.role
  tenant = "default"
}

# Use with data source
data "permitio_user" "example" {
  key = "user@example.com"
}

resource "permitio_role_assignment" "example" {
  user   = data.permitio_user.example.key
  role   = "viewer"
  tenant = "production"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `role` (String) Role key to assign
- `tenant` (String) Tenant key for scoped assignment
- `user` (String) User key to assign the role to

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the role assignment was created
- `environment_id` (String) Environment ID
- `id` (String) Unique identifier of the role assignment
- `organization_id` (String) Organization ID
- `project_id` (String) Project ID

## Import

Import role assignments using the format `user:role:tenant`:

```shell
terraform import permitio_role_assignment.example "user@example.com:admin:default"
```

Import examples:

```shell
# Import admin role assignment
terraform import permitio_role_assignment.admin "admin@company.com:admin:default"

# Import editor role in production tenant
terraform import permitio_role_assignment.editor "editor@company.com:editor:production"
```
